sourceMovie.title: Se7en
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Se7en works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Number 23
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Se7en clicked for you, The Number 23 (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Se7en?
faq.answer: Try The Number 23, Copycat, The Silence of the Lambs—each shares DNA with Se7en while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Se7en?
faq.answer: Se7en (1995) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Se7en worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
